Geddy Lucier
Senior Thesis
Regression Pipeline

This document is designed to create a fluid data pipeline to the algonquin/chicago citygate regression comparison

Data Load-in preamble
```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(dplyr) # Data Manipulation
library(lubridate) # Date manipulation
library(ggplot2) # Visualization
library(riem) # Weather api from airport observations
library(stargazer)


setwd("~/Documents/GitHub/Algonquin_NatGas/data/")

prices <- read_csv("prices_new.csv") # comes from Bloomberg Terminal. 
electricity_demand <- read_csv("electricity_demand.csv")
mix_load_df <- read_csv("mix_load_df.csv")
```
Initial Price and electricity cleaning 
```{r}
chicago <- prices[c(1,2)] %>% rename("Date" = "Date...1")
algonquin <- prices[c(3,4)]  %>% rename("Date" = "Date...3")

master_dates <- chicago %>%
  select("Date") %>%
  distinct()

prices <- chicago %>%
  left_join(algonquin, by = "Date")

start_date <- tail(chicago$Date, 1)
end_date <- head(chicago$Date,1)
```

Electricity
```{r}
### Check out Algonquin_NatGas/eia_dataload_template.R to load data
#source("/Users/geddylucier/Documents/GitHub/Algonquin_NatGas/eia_dataload.R")
elec <- electricity_demand %>%
  filter(timezone == "Eastern") 

#Split up by city
boston_elec <- elec %>% filter(city == "Boston")

algonquin <- algonquin %>% left_join(boston_elec, by = c("Date" = "date"))

chicago_elec <- elec %>% filter(city == "Chicago")

chicago <- chicago %>% left_join(chicago_elec, by = c("Date" = "date"))
```
Renewable Generation
```{r}
boston_mix <- mix_load_df %>% filter(respondent == "ISNE" & class == "green") #%>% select(date, pct_of_total) # Assume regional mix is similar to city mix

chicago_mix <- mix_load_df %>% filter(respondent == "PJM" & class == "green") #%>% select(date, pct_of_total) # Assume regional mix is similar to city mix

algonquin <- algonquin %>% left_join(boston_mix, by = c("Date" = "date"))

chicago <- chicago %>% left_join(chicago_mix, by =  c("Date" = "date"))

chicago
```


Weather Feature
```{r}
# #Boston 
# boston_weather <- riem_measures(
#   station = "BED", # Bedford Station, not enough Boston Data
#   date_start = start_date,
#   date_end = end_date) %>%
#   mutate(date = as.Date(valid)) %>%
#   group_by(date) %>%  # GROUP BY DATE FIRST
#   summarise(
#     temp_max = max(tmpf, na.rm = TRUE),
#     temp_min = min(tmpf, na.rm = TRUE),
#     temp_avg = mean(tmpf, na.rm = TRUE),
#     
#     HDD = pmax(0, 65 - temp_avg),
#     CDD = pmax(0, temp_avg - 65) ,
#     .groups = 'drop'
#   ) %>% select(date, HDD, CDD)
# 
#write_csv(boston_weather, file = "boston_weather.csv")
boston_weather <- read_csv("boston_weather.csv")


algonquin <- algonquin %>%
  left_join(boston_weather, by = c("Date" = "date"))


# #Chicago
# chicago_weather <- riem_measures(
#   station = "KORD",
#   date_start = start_date,
#   date_end = end_date) %>%
#   mutate(date = as.Date(valid)) %>%
#   group_by(date) %>%  # GROUP BY DATE FIRST
#   summarise(
#     temp_max = max(tmpf, na.rm = TRUE),
#     temp_min = min(tmpf, na.rm = TRUE),
#     temp_avg = mean(tmpf, na.rm = TRUE),
# 
# 
#     HDD = pmax(0, 65 - temp_avg),
#     CDD = pmax(0, temp_avg - 65) ,
#     .groups = 'drop'
#   ) %>% select(date, HDD, CDD)
# 
# write_csv(chicago_weather, file = "chicago_weather.csv")
chicago_weather <- read_csv("chicago_weather.csv")

chicago <- chicago %>%
  left_join(chicago_weather,  by = c("Date" = "date"))

```

Summary Stats
```{r}
boston_clean <- algonquin %>%
  select(boston, HDD, CDD, electricity_demand_mw, pct_of_total) %>%
  mutate(across(everything(), as.numeric)) %>%
  as.data.frame()

chicago_clean <- chicago %>%
  select(chicago, HDD, CDD, electricity_demand_mw, pct_of_total) %>%
  mutate(across(everything(), as.numeric)) %>%
  as.data.frame()

stargazer(boston_clean, chicago_clean,
          type             = "latex",
          title            = "Descriptive Statistics",
          digits           = 2,
          summary          = TRUE,
          summary.stat     = c("n", "mean", "sd", "min", "max"),
          covariate.labels = c("Price (\\$/MMBtu)",
                               "HDD",
                               "CDD",
                               "Electricity Demand (MW)",
                               "Renewable Share (\\%)"),
          column.labels    = c("Boston (Algonquin)", "Chicago"),
          column.separate  = c(5, 5),
          notes            = "Sample period 2020--2025. HDD and CDD computed using 65$^\\circ$F baseline.",
          notes.align      = "l",
          out              = "descriptive_stats.tex")
```


joint_df creation
```{r}
chicago <- chicago %>% rename("price" = "chicago") %>% select(Date, price, city, electricity_demand_mw, pct_of_total, HDD, CDD)

algonquin <- algonquin %>% rename("price" = "boston") %>% select(Date, price, city, electricity_demand_mw, pct_of_total, HDD, CDD)# need to make names consistent


joint_df <- rbind(chicago, algonquin) 
copy_df <- joint_df # make a copy with NAs
joint_df <- joint_df %>% drop_na()


colnames(joint_df)
```


Regression Data 
```{r}
summary(lm(price ~ factor(city) + HDD + electricity_demand_mw + CDD + pct_of_total, data = joint_df))
model <- (lm(price ~ city + HDD + electricity_demand_mw + CDD + pct_of_total, data = joint_df))

```

```{r}
stargazer(model,
          type             = "latex",
          title            = "Regression Results: Natural Gas Price Premium",
          dep.var.labels   = "Natural Gas Price (\\$/MMBtu)",
          covariate.labels = c("Chicago (vs. Boston)",
                               "Heating Degree Days (HDD)",
                               "Electricity Demand (MW)",      # matches position 3
                               "Cooling Degree Days (CDD)",    # matches position 4
                               "Renewable Share (\\% of Total Gen.)"),
          omit.stat        = c("ser", "f"),
          star.cutoffs     = c(0.1, 0.05, 0.01),
          digits           = 3,
          digit.separator  = "",
          column.sep.width = "10pt",
          font.size        = "small",
          notes            = c("Standard errors in parentheses.",
                               "765 observations dropped due to missingness.",
                               "Sample period: December 2019 -- December 2025."),
          notes.align      = "l",
          notes.append     = FALSE,
          label            = "tab:results",
          out              = "regression_results.tex")
```


Extensions

```{r}


boston_ext

```

```{r}
boston_mix <- mix_load_df %>%
  filter(respondent == "ISNE" & class == "green") %>%
  select(date, total_renewable, pct_of_total)

boston_df <- prices %>%
  select(Date,boston) %>% mutate(
  trade_day = ifelse(is.na(boston), 0, 1))


boston_fin <- master_dates %>%
  left_join(boston_weather, by = c("Date" = "date")) %>% 
  left_join(boston_mix, by = c("Date" = "date")) %>% 
  left_join(boston_df, by = "Date") %>% select(HDD, CDD, total_renewable, pct_of_total, trade_day)

colnames(boston_fin)
boston_fin
trade_day_model <- glm(trade_day ~  total_renewable + pct_of_total, data = boston_fin, family = binomial())

summary(trade_day_model)




```
LOGIT STARGAZER

```{r}
stargazer(trade_day_model,
          type             = "latex",
          title            = "Logistic Regression: Predictors of Algonquin Trading Day Formation",
          dep.var.labels   = "Trading Day (1 = Price Formed, 0 = No Index)",
          covariate.labels = c("Total Renewable Generation (MWh)",
                               "Renewable Share (\\% of Total Gen.)"),
          apply.coef       = exp,
          ci               = TRUE,
          ci.level         = 0.95,
          p.auto           = FALSE,
          star.cutoffs     = c(0.1, 0.05, 0.01),
          digits           = 4,
          digit.separator  = "",
          omit.stat        = c("aic", "ll", "bic"),
          font.size        = "small",
          notes            = c("Odds ratios reported with 95\\% confidence intervals.",
                               "Binomial GLM estimated on Boston (Algonquin) observations only.",
                               "Dependent variable equals 1 on days a daily price index formed.",
                               "Model selected on AIC over single-variable alternatives.",
                               "Sample period: December 2019 -- December 2025."),
          notes.align      = "l",
          notes.append     = FALSE,
          label            = "tab:tradeday",
          out              = "tradeday_results.tex")

```
